commands_run: bash scripts/agent-coord.sh claim form-native-arm64-object-floor "walk next Form-native JIT/object floor: emit and inspect macOS Mach-O plus Android ELF arm64 objects without clang as compiler or assembler"
cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-lower.fk form-stdlib/form-elf.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-elf-band.fk
failure_reason: Added a Form-native ELF64/aarch64 relocatable object wrapper and wired supported target rows to per-plane Form-native object emitters: macOS uses form-macho and Android uses form-elf. Focused ELF proof returns 31 four-way; target proof returns 8191 four-way; metal-plane audit writes Mach-O and ELF object bytes, and file(1) reports Mach-O 64-bit object arm64 plus ELF 64-bit LSB relocatable ARM aarch64. Rebased full Form validation returns fourth arm: 148 band(s) four-way with 566 ok and 0 divergent. This is a real Form-native object floor, not a full 100 percent Form-native BML/Hati/JIT replacement yet; broader BML/Hati and Form JIT C surfaces still use clang as oracle while Android's minimum JIT/object floor no longer depends on runtime clang.
